title: Explained | How India’s first hydrogen-powered train works
content: Unlike conventional diesel locomotives that burn fuel to generate mechanical power, a hydrogen train carries a small power plant onboard in the form of a Proton Exchange Membrane (PEM) fuel cell

title: Draft Apartment Bill gives flat owners stronger rights over common areas
content: In the proposed law, ownership of the land and common areas will remain with the flat owners collectively, and not with the association itself

title: ₹5 lakh bribe leads to arrest of MD of Bengaluru-based Karnataka Antibiotics and Pharmaceuticals Limited by CBI
content: Karnataka Antibiotics and Pharmaceuticals Limited (KAPL) Managing Director Anurag Danayak was allegedly caught red-handed taking the bribe from a company that is a service agent of KAPL, according to a CBI spokesperson

title: Mahasweta Devi at 100: Naveen Kishore remembers a life of writing as activism
content: Naveen Kishore, Mahasweta Devi’s long-time friend and poet-publisher, reflects on the phenomenon that she was and her literature of resistance

title: Kochi Corporation orders probe into spending of ₹6.32 crore for streetlight maintenance in 2022-23
content: A report on expenditure towards streetlight repair and maintenance tabled in council; a survey is to be conducted to verify number of LED lights that CSML claimed to have installed under City LED Street Light project last year and assess number of old lights that remain to be replaced, says Mayor

title: Avoid deploying Indian sailors on vessels moving via Strait of Hormuz: DGMA to shipping companies
content: The advisory came after the attacks this week on two vessels, in which 30 Indian seafarers were among a combined crew of 46

title: Daily ridership of women passengers on KSRTC Ordinary buses increase from 6.48 lakh to 12.12 lakh, says Kerala Minister
content: Despite the roll-out of the scheme on Ordinary buses, number of male passengers using these services has remained almost constant. This indicates that increase in women passengers has not come at the expense of other passengers, says C.P. John

title: Chhattisgarh passes Bill to boost business
content: The Bill brings 43 services offered by eight state departments under the risk-based approval framework, with provisions to add more services after approval by the Executive Council

title: Govt. lists five Bills for Monsoon Session, no mention of delimitation legislation
content: Government to table Prevention of Insults to National Honour (Amendment) Bill that will make the intentional insult of, or disruption during, the singing of Vande Mataram a punishable offence

title: NEET-UG 2026 results: 11.21 lakh candidates qualify in re-test; women outshine men
content: Two candidates, Aryan Gupta from Punjab and Panshul Bansal from Haryana, secured the highest score of 715 (out of 720 marks) in the re-examination; 19 candidates crossed the 700-mark threshold; of the top 138 candidates (scores above 690), 93% cracked the exam on their first attempt, with 99% of them aged between 17 and 19 years

title: Rot in KPSC recruitment runs deep
content: The recent scandal to hit the recruitment process in the Karnataka State Public Service Commission, with the Chairperson facing allegations of nepotism, is only the latest in a series over the last few decades. Sharath S. Srivatsa takes an overview of the system

title: Six murders, a dead suspect and a chilling trail gone cold
content: On July 10, six persons were killed across two homes in Daivalaguda, a peaceful farming village near Hyderabad. With investigations under way, the case raises uncomfortable questions about police procedure, anticipatory bail, victim protection and whether the tragedy could have been prevented

title: Former Maoists trade years of conflict for livelihood and a stable future
content: For many former Maoists, surrender only marks the beginning of a long journey. While they attempt to rebuild their lives through farming and family, rehabilitation remains a work in progress. Many continue to grapple with unemployment, inadequate skills, and challenges of reintegration, writes T. Appalanaidu

title: Mahasweta Devi at 100: Why her greatest legacy lies in the Adivasi movements she helped build
content: In Mahasweta Devi’s birth centenary year, and a decade since her death, a look at the writer’s enduring impact on grassroots movements through literature and activism

title: ‘Kafir screenshot’ case: order on SIT plea to cancel accused’s bail today
content: The Kozhikode Sessions Court is also expected to pronounce its order on the anticipatory bail plea filed by another DYFI leader, Ribesh Ramakrishnan, in connection with the case

title: BJP’s statewide protests against Omar’s J&K govt. to coincide with NC’s Jantar Mantar sit-in on Statehood
content: The BJP is preparing to organise protests over the J&K government’s outsourcing of jobs and will assemble outside the Civil Secretariat in Srinagar on July 20

title: CM Suvendu joins Rath Yatra in Kolkata; several BJP leaders join festivities
content: Suvendu Adhikari reiterates their plan to hand over mid-day meals to ISKCON, even as no official notification has been issued so far

title: SC dismisses plea by Karnataka Legislative Council Deputy Chairman against recounting of votes
content: The appeal was heard by a three-judge comprising Chief Justice of India Surya Kant, Justice Vipul M. Pancholi, and Justice Joymalya Bagchi

title: Trinamool MP Samirul Islam calls on family who was pushed into Bangladesh
content: Four Bengali migrants from Birbhum who were detained by Delhi Police last year and pushed into Bangladesh were brought back to India on July 8, 2026 after a year-long legal battle. It was Samirul Islam who took up the cause of the migrant workers and provided help to the family

title: Dates with devotion: The division in Puri’s Rath Yatra celebrations
content: The Jagannath Temple in Puri holds its Rath Yatra every year on a date prescribed by its centuries-old ritual calendar. A branch of ISKCON, however, stages international events of the procession on dates that differ from the original, citing practical reasons. The divergence has sparked a controversy, with many in Puri viewing it as a challenge to the temple’s authority and its religious identity, finds Satyasundar Barik

title: Higher education Bill may lead to concentration of powers in single central regulator: House panel
content: The Bill was introduced in the Lok Sabha in December last year, following which it was referred to a joint committee of Parliament

title: Mahasweta Devi’s best books: grandson Tathagata Bhattacharya recommends five must-reads
content: The writer-activist once told her grandson that she was appreciated for the wrong reasons. Recalling that conversation, he lists five works that he believes are the most powerful

title: BMTC plans higher non-fare revenue with QR code-based ads on bus seatbacks
content: The proposed QR displays will measure 10 cm x 10 cm and will be placed on the rear side of passenger seats, according to officials.

title: After ambushes, CRPF tells forces in Manipur to use armoured vehicles, avoid sudden deployment
content: An official said the forces have been told to take action against armed miscreants and “anyone found with a gun is to be arrested or fired upon, if they do not surrender”

title: Supreme Court judgments say state has a duty to preserve life of person on indefinite fast without disrupting right to dissent
content: The Union government has responded to activist Sonam Wanghuk’s ongoing fasting at Jantar Mantar with silence for the past 19 days even as an alarmed Delhi High Court asserted on July 16 that the ‘life of any citizen is precious’

title: Monsoon season power crisis: Power curbs likely to continue in Kerala
content: Electricity Minister Sunny Joseph said that the State is currently facing a 500 MW to 600 MW deficit in availability

title: Supreme Court orders States to frame policy for early release of elderly, terminally ill prisoners
content: The top court ordered States and Union Territories to frame such a policy within three months in coordination with the respective State Legal Services Authorities

title: Study explores potential of abandoned quarries in city to augment water sources
content: Researchers, including those from IIT-M, are assessing nearly 25 quarries in city’s southern fringes; the study focuses on evaluating the feasibility of linking them to the nearest water supply network

title: As Wangchuk’s health worsens, Delhi High Court orders regular monitoring
content: Wangchuk has lost over 9 kg after starting hunger strike and his body is consuming muscles and his organs may be next, says doctor; govt. assures High Court of necessary medical intervention

title: CB-CID begins probe into registration of Palani math land
content: A team of 15 police officers, led by Deputy Superintendent of Police Ajay Thangam, conducted inquiries in Palani.

title: Oil marketing companies yet to issue new LPG connections to domestic consumers
content: Many say they have been waiting since March, when the conflict began in West Asia. They complain that cooking with induction stoves is not always convenient for them

title: How chance discovery of Rs 40,000 in loo helped blow lid off Ayodhya Ram temple theft
content: A chance discovery of concealed cash led to an investigation into donation theft. Alerted by a gatekeeper, temple trustees initiated an internal inquiry into the matter. Police launched a probe and recovered nearly eighty lakh rupees from multiple locations. Accused individuals provided disclosures which aided further recoveries of stolen money. A special investigation team is now reconstructing the entire sequence of events.

title: 'Hasn't scored in two games': India coach's verdict on Rohit Sharma amid poor form
content: Rohit's struggles were visible in the second ODI in Cardiff on Thursday, where England won by four wickets to level the three-match series 1-1. The opener never looked settled, scoring just 10 runs off 18 balls by the end of the Powerplay even as India raced to 61/1. He eventually made 26 off 47 balls before being dismissed while attempting a sweep against Will Jacks.

title: Mark Cuban's 'warning' to Americans: AI risks making US healthcare worse
content: Mark Cuban cautions that artificial intelligence alone will not solve healthcare issues. He believes insurers will use AI to hinder doctor and patient treatment approvals. Cuban notes that conglomerates already employ AI to manipulate contracts and delay payments. He states that companies lack knowledge of their actual healthcare purchase costs. Cuban advocates for disintermediation to eliminate unnecessary middlemen in healthcare.

title: NEET UG 2026 AIR 1 Aryan Gupta shares success mantra, advice for aspirants
content: The NTA has announced the NEET UG 2026 results, with over 11.21 lakh candidates qualifying for medical admissions. Punjab's Aryan Gupta, who secured a joint AIR 1 with 715 marks, credited his success to 16–17 hours of daily study and perseverance. Aspiring to become an oncologist, he urged future aspirants to keep working hard and never lose hope.

title: Jeff Bezos donates $10m to LA that city's mayor says is about investing in culture
content: Jeff Bezos' Earth Fund has granted $10.1 million for Watts Towers Arts Center Campus improvements. This investment will expand usable park space and introduce new cooling features. The project aims to strengthen the campus as a cultural hub before the 2028 Olympics. Los Angeles Mayor Karen Bass welcomed the significant milestone for the community. This funding advances commitments to green space and climate resilience efforts.

title: 'China stole data of 220m US voters': Trump blames Beijing as he releases files on 2020 polls
content: President Donald Trump alleged China compromised 220 million American voter files. He announced declassification of intelligence documents regarding election interference. Trump claimed intelligence officials concealed this information from him and Congress. He stated that election systems are vulnerable and easily compromised. This announcement comes as Republicans prepare for upcoming midterm elections.

title: California may get $65M to leave 65 billion gallons in Lake Mead
content: Historically low water levels are plaguing Lake Mead, affecting millions in various states and Mexico. To combat this, federal funds will support efforts to keep water in the reservoir this year, coupled with initiatives aimed at conserving agricultural water annually. While these measures are crucial for now, achieving lasting change will necessitate cooperation among Colorado River Basin states regarding future water management.

title: Buying a phone without giving up your identity may soon become impossible in the US
content: The FCC is considering a proposal for identity verification for phone services.  This rule would require carriers to collect customer names and addresses.  Privacy advocates warn this could exclude vulnerable populations from phone access.  They also cite concerns about data security and past telecom breaches.  Critics argue overseas robocalls will remain unaffected by domestic ID checks.

title: Top stocks to buy: Stock recommendations for today - check list
content: Stock market recommendations: Adani Power, and IndusInd Bank - Bajaj Broking Research has recommended these two stocks to buy today, July 17, 2026, with a one-month horizon for the target price to be achieved. Let’s take a look:

title: Vivek Oberoi reveals he invested in over 30 companies, exited 11 with returns of up to 300%
content: While many know Vivek Oberoi as an actor, he has quietly built a significant presence in the investment world. In a recent interview, the actor spoke about his journey from learning the value of money as a child to creating a family office focused on generational wealth, impact investing and backing promising entrepreneurs.

title: Gigi Hadid reportedly deepened Blake Lively's fallout with Taylor Swift
content: Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce kept the drama away from their New York City wedding as they invited pretty much everyone from Hollywood and the NFL but Blake Lively and Ryan Reynolds, sparking chaos among many. While this has sparked a mixed reaction among fans, it seems supermodel Gigi Hadid might have made things worse for Blake Lively.

title: Who is Fátima Bosch? The Miss Universe linked to Jude Bellingham rumors
content: Miss Universe 2025 winner Fatima Bosch has attracted worldwide attention after social media users associated her with England national football team star Jude Bellingham. Bosch has denied meeting her, but the rumors have sparked a lot of interest in her life, her education, her career and her amazing journey from Mexico to becoming one of the world’s most recognizable beauty queens.

title: Marcello Hernandez's Tiger Woods joke steals the spotlight at the ESPYs
content: Marcello Hernandez delivered some of the biggest moments at the 2026 ESPYs with jokes aimed at Tiger Woods and Bill Belichick. Hernandez referenced Woods’ ongoing DUI case and poked fun at Belichick’s relationship with Jordon Hudson. The comedian’s sharp monologue drew strong reactions from the audience and became one of the night’s main talking points.

title: Rohit Sharma no more automatic selection after Lord's ODI
content: The Indian team management and selectors had initially considered giving Yashasvi Jaiswal a full series in England but opted to persist with Rohit Sharma, hoping he would rediscover his form. Sources indicate that both the selection committee and team management have conveyed their views on Rohit's future to the BCCI. It remains to be seen whether the board's leadership or the selectors will now initiate discussions with him.

title: FBI arrests gangster Nitish Kaushal days after adding him to most wanted list
content: The FBI arrested Indian-origin Nitish Kaushal in Vermont as part of a crackdown. Kaushal is wanted for alleged involvement in murder and drug trafficking. He carried out violent acts for the Bhagwanpuria organised crime group. This arrest follows Operation Hard Ball targeting the Bhagwanpuria gang internationally. Federal prosecutors also charged Lawrence Bishnoi and Goldy Brar with ordering an assassination.

title: Unease in NCP after signs of NCP (SP) tilting towards NDA
content: NCP leaders Praful Patel and Sunil Tatkare met Chief Minister Devendra Fadnavis without informing Sunetra Pawar. Speculations are rife about NCP (SP) potentially aligning with the NDA government. Jayant Patil's meetings with BJP leaders intensified these ongoing political discussions. Sunetra Pawar's faction faces concerns about losing influence if NCP (SP) joins the alliance. Discussions regarding the finance portfolio and party leadership challenges are ongoing.

title: Where's hitman? Rohit Sharma's form a growing concern for India's ODI WC plans
content: Rohit Sharma's struggles with timing, tempo and confidence have continued in the first two ODIs against England. With just 37 runs, prolonged dot-ball phases and visible discomfort against pace, the veteran opener's place in India's long-term ODI plans is beginning to invite serious scrutiny ahead of the 2027 World Cup.

title: Man uses dog's leash to strangle pregnant wife before dying by suicide
content: A Bengaluru carpenter strangled his pregnant wife and then died by suicide in their rented home. The incident came to light after the house owner heard loud fighting and then silence. Separately, a 20-year-old housekeeping staffer was beaten to death by relatives over a match. The youth was attacked after a volleyball game and succumbed to his injuries later. Police are investigating both incidents and searching for the accused individuals.

title: Acquitted 45 yrs on, after serving life term; murder in 1977, conviction 1981, clean chit 2026
content: The Supreme Court acquitted three surviving accused in a 49-year-old murder case.  This decision came after the accused had served life imprisonment sentences.  The court found serious infirmities in the prosecution's case and witness testimonies.  Inconsistencies in witness accounts led to granting the benefit of doubt.  The prosecution failed to establish guilt beyond reasonable doubt, leading to acquittal.

title: LeBron James to Miami Heat? NBA free agency hint links him with Giannis
content: Pat Riley has already pulled off one blockbuster. Now he's not hiding that he wants another. The Miami Heat president spent Thursday's introductory press conference for Giannis Antetokounmpo talking about the trade that brought a two-time MVP to South Beach, only to pivot, unprompted, to the free agent still floating out there: LeBron James.

title: Crude heads for biggest weekly gain in months amid intensifying Middle East crisis
content: Oil prices surged as escalating US-Iran military tensions raised supply disruption concerns. Brent crude traded at $85.34 and WTI crude at $80.08 per barrel. Recent US strikes aimed to degrade Iranian military capabilities, while Iran retaliated against regional allies. Renewed hostilities revived fears of a broader conflict and threats to the Strait of Hormuz. Iran warned of widespread infrastructure damage if US threats to strike its facilities are carried out.

title: NEET-UG results declared: 2 score 715/720, 19 cross 700
content: NEET-UG 2026 results revealed higher top scores following a nationwide retest. Aryan Gupta from Punjab secured the top position with a score of 715. Over 93% of candidates scoring above 690 were first-time test-takers. Women accounted for over 58% of the qualified candidates this year. The National Testing Agency declared results to keep medical admissions on track.

title: Jude Bellingham's girlfriend speaks out after months of online abuse
content: In March 2025, Jude Bellingham's girlfriend Ashlyn Castro addressed months of online trolling and false rumors about her dating history in an emotional TikTok video. She revealed she had only been in three relationships over eight years and described the relentless harassment as overwhelming. Castro also said she hoped to use her platform to raise awareness about mental health after sharing her deeply personal experience.

title: Did MS Dhoni predict Spain vs Argentina WC final 10 yrs ago? Old Insta post goes viral
content: Argentina's World Cup semifinal victory has caused an old Instagram post by MS Dhoni to resurface.  The 2016 picture showed Dhoni playing PlayStation with teammates, captioned "Spain vs Argentina".  This post gained attention as Argentina will now face Spain in the World Cup final.  Dhoni was also seen watching India's recent white-ball tour matches in England.  Argentina aims to defend their World Cup title against Spain in New Jersey.

title: Vivek Oberoi reacts to Wangchuk's hunger strike: 'Yaar main abhineta hu, neta nahi hu'
content: Vivek Oberoi has reacted to Sonam Wangchuk's ongoing hunger strike at Delhi's Jantar Mantar, saying he prefers to stay away from political debates but believes that people should have the freedom to express their views in a healthy democracy. While he did not comment on the specific demands or the issues surrounding the protest, the actor said he sees himself as someone who observes and learns from what is happening around him.

title: Bumrah scripts never-seen-before England record with first-ball stunner
content: Ben Duckett became the first England batter dismissed on the opening ball against India. Jasprit Bumrah provided India with an ideal start in the chase of 234. India posted a below-par 233 after a middle-order collapse in their innings. Virat Kohli and Shreyas Iyer scored 65 and 66 respectively. England's pace attack triggered India's collapse, losing four wickets quickly.

title: 'Engines didn't start': SpaceX aborts Starship's 13th test just before liftoff
content: SpaceX's Starship rocket aborted its thirteenth flight test just before liftoff.  The automated launch scrub occurred moments before the planned liftoff from Texas.  Raptor engines ignited but shut down shortly afterward, preventing the launch.  Engineers will investigate the cause and determine a new launch attempt.  This marks another delay for the powerful reusable launch system.

title: Long-term exposure to air pollution could lead to dementia, warns WHO
content: New WHO guidelines suggest up to 45% of dementia cases are preventable.  Tackling risk factors like high blood pressure and air pollution is advised.  Healthy lifestyles and managing chronic diseases significantly influence brain health.  The guidelines recommend regular physical activity and quitting tobacco use.  Routine vitamin and omega-3 supplements are not advised without diagnosed deficiency.

title: Skyroot Aerospace to launch India’s first private orbital rocket on Saturday
content: India's private space sector is poised for a significant advancement this Saturday. Skyroot Aerospace will launch the Vikram-1, its first orbital-class rocket. This historic mission, named Mission Aagaman, will lift off from Sriharikota. The rocket carries technology demonstration payloads and unique artwork into space. This launch marks a major milestone for India's burgeoning private space industry.

title: PF for self-employed, workers in unorganised sector on way
content: Employees Provident Fund Organisation is developing a universal provident fund scheme.  This new plan will cover millions in the unorganised sector and self-employed individuals.  Subscribers can contribute daily or annually with tax benefits on their savings.  The agency is studying global models to finalize the basic framework for this initiative.  This self-financing scheme aims to extend retirement savings beyond the current EPFO network.

title: Lana Del Rey's wedding absence fuels fresh Taylor Swift friendship rumors
content: Lana Del Rey did not attend Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce's July 3 wedding, choosing instead to celebrate the Fourth of July in Louisiana and attend YouTuber Oli Abbas' birthday. Her absence sparked renewed speculation about her friendship with Swift, but Del Rey recently praised the singer's success, suggesting there is no public sign of a falling out despite ongoing fan rumors.

title: Unite to fight terror, organised crime, Ajit Doval urges Bimstec
content: NSA Ajit Doval chaired the Bimstec national security chiefs meeting in New Delhi.  The grouping discussed practical solutions to combat terrorism and organized crime.  Security chiefs also endorsed principles for maritime law enforcement interactions at sea.  Doval stressed closer regional cooperation to tackle evolving security challenges effectively.  Bimstec unites dynamic regions with significant global population and economic influence.

title: Call all-party meet to discuss delimitation bill: Kharge to PM Modi
content: Congress chief Mallikarjun Kharge requested an all-party meeting with Prime Minister Modi. He seeks details on the revised Constitution (131st Amendment) Bill. Reports suggest the government plans to reintroduce the delimitation bill soon. Congress believes the government lacks the required majority for constitutional amendments. The party remains in touch with opposition parties regarding the bill.

title: Fema a civil law, RBI alone can compound offences, says ED
content: The Enforcement Directorate clarified Fema's role as civil legislation for voluntary compliance. Reserve Bank of India is the competent authority for compounding eligible contraventions. The ED issues a no-objection certificate to the central bank for compounding offenses. RBI recently closed a Fema case against Apothecon Pharmaceuticals after a penalty. This action follows master directions prescribing a compounding matrix for determining amounts.

title: Army chief visits military base near Siliguri Corridor to review operational preparedness
content: Army chief General Dhiraj Seth reviewed operational preparedness of Eastern Command formations. He visited IV Corps, XXXIII Corps, and III Corps during his tour. General Seth was briefed on security situations and capability development initiatives. He commended formations for internal security and disaster relief contributions. The chief urged troops to remain mission-focused and enhance inter-agency synergy.

title: US launches fresh strikes on Iran; state TV says Bandar Abbas hit by projectiles
content: United States forces conducted a new wave of strikes against Iran on Thursday. These attacks marked the fifth consecutive night of military operations by the US. The strikes targeted Iran's military capabilities and facilities in Bandar Abbas. This escalation follows recent attacks on commercial shipping in the Strait of Hormuz. Relations between Washington and Tehran have sharply deteriorated amid these confrontations.

title: 'Not a blanket bar': Supreme Court clarifies bulldozer order
content: The Supreme Court clarified its prior ruling on demolitions. Bulldozers can remove illegal constructions and encroachments on public land. This action requires strict adherence to municipal laws and procedures. Individual contempt petitions will be handled by jurisdictional High Courts. Fact-finding exercises will ascertain if authorities acted arbitrarily in demolitions.

title: Stock Market LIVE Updates, Sensex Today: Sensex Likely To Open Flat, Asian Markets Decline
content: Stock Market, Sensex, Share Market, Nifty LIVE Updates: Oil prices jump nearly 12% this week as US-Iran strikes fuel Gulf supply fears.

title: "Nightmare": Trump Claims China Interfered In 2020 US Polls, Beijing Responds
content: More than 60 lawsuits by Trump and his allies produced no ruling establishing fraud capable of changing the outcome of the 2020 election, while recounts, audits, and his own Justice Department found none.

title: Gujarat's Lion Paradox: Why Big Cat Boom Poses A New Test For Authorities
content: Prides of lions are increasingly stepping out of their traditional sanctuary boundaries, wandering into agricultural fields, open grasslands, and coastal areas.

title: US-Iran War News LIVE: 7 Killed As US Launches 6th Consecutive Night Of Strikes Against Iran
content: US-Iran War Live Updates: A fifth of all traded crude oil and natural gas passed through the Strait of Hormuz before the war, when it was open to all without tolls.

title: Covid Cases Rising Again: Here's What Your Health Insurance Won't Cover
content: Expenses such as pulse oximeters, masks, sanitisers, supplements and even some medicines may not be covered unless the policy.

title: Team Trump Alters Rule To Deny Green Cards To Immigrants Using Public Benefits
content: The Trump administration is reviving a rule that could deny green cards to immigrants who use public benefits that could include food stamps, Medicaid, housing vouchers and others.

title: Nitish Kaushal, Member Of Jaggu Bhagwanpuria Gang, Arrested In US
content: Indian-origin gangster Nitish Kaushal's arrest comes two days after FBI put him to its "wanted" list.

title: US Attacks Iran In Tit-For-Tat Strikes, Hits Airport, Railway Station, Bridges
content: US strikes overnight hit an airport and two bridges in southern Iran near the Strait of Hormuz, Iranian state media said Friday.

title: She Knew He Wasn't Real. One Woman's Romance Scam Story
content: She knew somewhere in the back of her mind that Donnie didn't exist. She also knew that before she hit the road again, in spite of all logic and months of warnings from everyone in her life, she'd send the money anyway.

title: Congress Worker At Rahul Gandhi's Dehradun Event Killed After Iron Rod Falls On Him
content: A 65-year-old Congress worker died after an iron rod from a tent erected for Rahul Gandhi's scheduled event fell on him at the Bannu School grounds in Dehradun on Thursday evening, a party leader said.

title: US Announces Visa Curbs On Members Linked To "Far-Left Terrorist" Groups
content: The United States on Thursday launched a scathing attack on left-wing extremists, with Secretary of State Marco Rubio calling for global cooperation to fight a "new wave of this old evil."

title: SpaceX Starship Rocket Aborts Before Liftoff In 13th Flight Test Attempt
content: SpaceX's Starship rocket triggered a last-second abort before liftoff for its 13th flight test from Texas on Thursday, postponing the mission by at least 24 hours with the company studying what likely caused the automated scrub.

title: US Immigrant Agent In Maine Shooting Has History Of Violent Behavior: Family
content: The Immigration and Customs Enforcement officer who shot a Colombian man in Maine this week is an Army veteran who has struggled with serious mental health issues since early childhood, close relatives told.

title: Philippines Condemns Chinese Media's Video Depicting Filipinos As Monkeys
content: China Daily's AI-generated video was posted on July 10, coinciding with Philippine events marking the 10th anniversary of a landmark arbitral ruling that invalidated China's sweeping claims in the South China Sea.

title: Andhra Pradesh Reports 4 Covid-19 Deaths, 12 Cases In Less Than A Month
content: Andhra Pradesh Health Commissioner G Veerapandian on Thursday said 12 people were infected with Covid-19 virus across the state between June 26 and July 16.

title: Trump Media To Sell High Speed Access To Truth Social Posts
content: Donald Trump's media company said Thursday it planned to sell faster access to market-moving social media posts by the US president.

title: US Forces Continue "Wave Of Strikes" Against Iran For 6th Consecutive Night
content: According to the US Central Command (CENTCOM), the attack was aimed at further degrading Iran's military capabilities, as Tehran poses a threat to commercial vessels transiting through the Strait of Hormuz, which Washington claims.

title: Xi Jinping To Present China's AI Dream At Major Conference Today
content: President Xi Jinping presents China's vision for the development and regulation of artificial intelligence on Friday, at a major conference showcasing cutting-edge technology he hopes will soon rival that of the United States.

title: Odisha Government Confirms 2 Deaths, Denies Stampede At Puri Rath Yatra
content: The Odisha government has confirmed the deaths of two devotees but denied reports of any stampede-like incident during the annual Rath Yatra festival.

title: "Crude And Arrogant": Brazil Hits Out At Marco Rubio Over Remarks On Lula
content: Brazil's Foreign Minister Mauro Vieira on Thursday accused his US counterpart Marco Rubio of "attacking" President Luiz Inacio Lula da Silva in a "crude and arrogant" manner when commenting on a new tariff decision.

title: Trump admin limits students visa tenure to fixed period of 4 yrs, limits academic flexibility
content: Lubna Kably is a senior editor, who focuses on various policies and legislation. In particular, she writes extensively on immigration and tax policies. The Indian diaspora is the largest in the world; through her articles she demystifies the immigration-policy related developments in select countries for outbound students, job aspirants and employees. She also analyses the impact of Income-tax and GST related developments for individuals and business entities. Read More

title: Govt plans bill to punish those who insult, block Vande Mataram singing
content: the Prevention of Insults to National Honour (Amendment) bill will be introduced in Lok Sabha . it comes after guidelines made playing or singing the national song mandatory at official events where national anthem 'Jana Gana Mana' is played .
